title: The development of an American culture
description: Test your skills on how American culture changed from 1800 to 1848.
topic_path: The development of an American culture | 4.9
The development of an American culture

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The War has renewed and reinstated the national feelings which the Revolution had given and which were daily lessened. The people have now more general objects of attachment with which their pride and political opinions are connected. They are more American; they feel and act more like a nation; and I hope that the permanency of the Union is thereby better secured.”

>-Source: Albert Gallatin, in a letter to Matthew Lyon, 1816

**The excerpt provided is best understood in relation to which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The War of 1812
The Civil War
The Mexican-American War
The American Revolution

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The War has renewed and reinstated the national feelings which the Revolution had given and which were daily lessened. The people have now more general objects of attachment with which their pride and political opinions are connected. They are more American; they feel and act more like a nation; and I hope that the permanency of the Union is thereby better secured.”

>-Source: Albert Gallatin, in a letter to Matthew Lyon, 1816

**The author’s remarks in the excerpt most directly reflected which of the following developments during the early nineteenth century?**	

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the emergence of a national identity
the spread of a wave of religious revivalism
the realignment of national political parties by regional lines
the end of US efforts at territorial expansion

assessment_items.question_content:
> “The War has renewed and reinstated the national feelings which the Revolution had given and which were daily lessened. The people have now more general objects of attachment with which their pride and political opinions are connected. They are more American; they feel and act more like a nation; and I hope that the permanency of the Union is thereby better secured.”

>-Source: Albert Gallatin, in a letter to Matthew Lyon, 1816

**Which of the following most directly undermines the author’s assertions?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Missouri Compromise
The Era of Good Feelings
The Louisiana Purchase
The Monroe Doctrine

assessment_items.question_content:
> “From the listless repose of the place, and the peculiar character of its inhabitants, who are descendants from the original Dutch settlers, this sequestered glen has long been known by the name of SLEEPY HOLLOW, and its rustic lads are called the Sleepy Hollow Boys throughout all the neighboring country. A drowsy, dreamy influence seems to hang over the land, and to pervade the very atmosphere. Some say that the place was bewitched by a high German doctor, during the early days of the settlement; others, that an old Indian chief, the prophet or wizard of his tribe, held his pow-wows there before the country was discovered by Master Hendrick Hudson . . . . 

> “The dominant spirit, however, that haunts this enchanted region, and seems to be commander-in-chief of all the powers of the air, is the apparition of a figure on horseback without a head. It is said by some to be the ghost of a Hessian trooper, whose head had been carried away by a cannon-ball, in some nameless battle during the revolutionary war; and who is ever and anon seen by the country folk hurrying along in the gloom of night . . .”  

>-Source: Washington Irving, The Legend of Sleepy Hollow, 1820

**The excerpt best serves as evidence for which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the development of an American national culture
the emergence of the Second Great Awakening
the founding of the Democratic Party
the growing sectional tensions over slavery

assessment_items.question_content:
> “From the listless repose of the place, and the peculiar character of its inhabitants, who are descendants from the original Dutch settlers, this sequestered glen has long been known by the name of SLEEPY HOLLOW, and its rustic lads are called the Sleepy Hollow Boys throughout all the neighboring country. A drowsy, dreamy influence seems to hang over the land, and to pervade the very atmosphere. Some say that the place was bewitched by a high German doctor, during the early days of the settlement; others, that an old Indian chief, the prophet or wizard of his tribe, held his pow-wows there before the country was discovered by Master Hendrick Hudson . . . . 

> “The dominant spirit, however, that haunts this enchanted region, and seems to be commander-in-chief of all the powers of the air, is the apparition of a figure on horseback without a head. It is said by some to be the ghost of a Hessian trooper, whose head had been carried away by a cannon-ball, in some nameless battle during the revolutionary war; and who is ever and anon seen by the country folk hurrying along in the gloom of night . . .”  

>-Source: Washington Irving, The Legend of Sleepy Hollow, 1820

**A historian would most likely use this passage to illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the regional cultural sensibilities that influenced American culture
the British government’s decision to hire Hessian mercenaries
Henry Hudson’s exploration of New York Harbor
religious beliefs among residents of upstate New York

assessment_items.question_content:

A drowsy, dreamy influence seems to hang over the land, and to pervade the very atmosphere. Some say that the place was bewitched by a high German doctor, during the early days of the settlement; others, that an old Indian chief, the prophet or wizard of his tribe, held his pow-wows there before the country was discovered by Master Hendrick Hudson . . . .

It is said by some to be the ghost of a Hessian trooper, whose head had been carried away by a cannon-ball, in some nameless battle during the revolutionary war; and who is ever and anon seen by the country folk hurrying along in the gloom of night . . .”  

>-Source: Washington Irving, The Legend of Sleepy Hollow, 1820

**The language and themes of the excerpt were most directly inspired by the:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: American Renaissance, a movement which included the first American art and literature.
Mexican-American War, a conflict that garnered broad support among American writers.
Declaration of Independence, which led to the American Revolution.
Hartford Convention, a gathering of New England Federalists who opposed the War of 1812.

assessment_items.question_content:
![A painting depicting a valley. On the left side, the painting depicts untamed land underneath a gloomy storm cloud. On the right side, there is a peaceful, pastoral landscape meant to show how humankind has tamed nature. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/772e377ef9e512a67a498a27cc654286.jpg)

>-Source: Thomas Cole, *The Oxbow,* [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Thomas_Cole,_The_Oxbow.jpg), 1836

**The Romantic tradition of American landscape painting helped reinforce which popular idea from the nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Manifest Destiny
Temperance
Nativism
Abolitionism

assessment_items.question_content:
![A painting depicting a valley. On the left side, the painting depicts untamed land underneath a gloomy storm cloud. On the right side, there is a peaceful, pastoral landscape meant to show how humankind has tamed nature.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/772e377ef9e512a67a498a27cc654286.jpg)

>-Source: Thomas Cole, *The Oxbow,* [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Thomas_Cole,_The_Oxbow.jpg), 1836

**The painting can best be used as evidence for which of the following US historical trends that took place during the early nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the impact of Romantic beliefs on American art
the efforts by American artists to develop an identity distinct from British culture
the influence of Neoclassical art on American artists
the development and cultivation of American Impressionism

assessment_items.question_content:
![A painting depicting a valley. On the left side, the painting depicts untamed land underneath a gloomy storm cloud. On the right side, there is a peaceful, pastoral landscape meant to show how humankind has tamed nature.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/772e377ef9e512a67a498a27cc654286.jpg)

>-Source: Thomas Cole, *The Oxbow,* [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Thomas_Cole,_The_Oxbow.jpg), 1836

**Which of the following statements best describes how art during the early 1800s contrasted with art from the late 1700s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It focused more on landscapes and less on portraits of individuals.
It focused more on portraying stories of civic duty and less on backgrounds.
It emphasized creating portraits of major American heroes and American success in battles.
It emphasized capturing the beauty of everyday life through loose brushstrokes and bright colors.

assessment_items.question_content:
“Classical revival architecture associated with Thomas Jefferson and Benjamin Latrobe had rested on the theory that forms were beautiful in themselves and that architecture should display the principles of simplicity, harmony, and proportion; . . . borrowing from British [[☃ definition 1]] theorists like Archibald Alison, now argued that structural forms were beautiful in terms of the thoughts that they raised in the mind of the viewer. Thus gothic architecture, which was popularized in the works of Byron and Sir Walter Scott, became emblematic of the ideals of an earlier Christian age.” 

>-Source: Clifford E. Clark, Jr., “Domestic Architecture as an Index to Social History: The Romantic Revival and the Cult of Domesticity in America, 1840-1870,” *The Journal of Interdisciplinary History,* 1976

**According to the passage, which of the following best explains the most important effect that the romantic movement had on the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: concerned with the appreciation of beauty

choices.content: architecture found inspiration from medieval architecture
architecture that focused on the use of columns and geometric forms
literature focused on the supernatural and sensational
literature focused on portraying American war heroes

assessment_items.question_content:
“Classical revival architecture associated with Thomas Jefferson and Benjamin Latrobe had rested on the theory that forms were beautiful in themselves and that architecture should display the principles of simplicity, harmony, and proportion; . . . borrowing from British [[☃ definition 1]] theorists like Archibald Alison, now argued that structural forms were beautiful in terms of the thoughts that they raised in the mind of the viewer. Thus gothic architecture, which was popularized in the works of Byron and Sir Walter Scott, became emblematic of the ideals of an earlier Christian age.” 

>-Source: Clifford E. Clark, Jr., “Domestic Architecture as an Index to Social History: The Romantic Revival and the Cult of Domesticity in America, 1840-1870,” *The Journal of Interdisciplinary History,* 1976

**Which of the following contributed most directly to the trend described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: concerned with the appreciation of beauty

choices.content: the spread of Romantic beliefs from Europe
the rise of transcendentalism
the dissemination of Enlightenment ideas
the diffusion of Second Great Awakening ideals

assessment_items.question_content:
“Classical revival architecture associated with Thomas Jefferson and Benjamin Latrobe had rested on the theory that forms were beautiful in themselves and that architecture should display the principles of simplicity, harmony, and proportion; . . . borrowing from British [[☃ definition 1]] theorists like Archibald Alison, now argued that structural forms were beautiful in terms of the thoughts that they raised in the mind of the viewer. Thus gothic architecture, which was popularized in the works of Byron and Sir Walter Scott, became emblematic of the ideals of an earlier Christian age.” 

>-Source: Clifford E. Clark, Jr., “Domestic Architecture as an Index to Social History: The Romantic Revival and the Cult of Domesticity in America, 1840-1870,” *The Journal of Interdisciplinary History,* 1976

**The developments described in the excerpt best illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: concerned with the appreciation of beauty

choices.content: how liberal social ideas from Europe and Romantic beliefs influenced philosophy and literature in the United States
how Americans combined European influences and regional cultures to form a new national culture
how the rise of democratic and individualistic beliefs inspired the rise of social and moral reform movements
how regional cultures developed as people migrated to new areas around the United States
